linked_lists.name: Linked Lists (Singly & Doubly)
sub_items: Singly Linked Lists (append, prepend, insert, remove)

graphs.name: Graphs
sub_items: Adjacency Matrix, Adjacency List, Edge List

problems.title: Reverse a String
problems.statement: Given a string `s`, return the reversed string. You must solve this in O(n) time. Aim for O(1) extra space if using an in-place approach on a mutable buffer.
constraints: s consists of printable ASCII characters
Target space complexity: O(1) extra space (in-place) or O(n) if returning a new string

solution.approach: Use two pointers swapping characters from both ends toward the center, or build a new reversed string.

problems.title: Merge Sorted Arrays
problems.statement: You are given two sorted integer arrays `nums1` and `nums2`. Merge them into a single sorted array and return it.
constraints: nums1 and nums2 are sorted in non-decreasing order
Target space complexity: O(n + m) for the output array

solution.approach: Use two pointers to compare the smallest remaining elements from each array and append the smaller one.

problems.title: First Recurring Character
problems.statement: Given a string, return the first character that appears more than once when scanning left to right. If no character repeats, return `null`.
constraints: Target space complexity: O(k) where k is the number of unique characters

examples.explanation: c is the first character seen again.

solution.approach: Track seen characters in a hash set. Return the first character already in the set.

problems.title: Reverse a Linked List
problems.statement: Given the head of a singly linked list, reverse the list and return the new head. Input is provided as a array of values for convenience.
constraints: Target space complexity: O(1) extra space

solution.approach: Iterate with three pointers: previous, current, and next. Rewire current.next to previous at each step.

problems.title: Implement Queue using Stacks
problems.statement: Implement a queue class `MyQueue` using only stack operations (`push`, `pop`, `peek`, `empty`). Support `enqueue(x)`, `dequeue()`, `peek()`, and `is_empty()`.
constraints: Amortized O(1) time per operation is acceptable
You may only use LIFO stack behavior internally

solution.approach: Use an inbox stack for enqueue and an outbox stack for dequeue/peek. Transfer elements only when outbox is empty.

problems.title: Validate a Binary Search Tree
problems.statement: Given a binary tree represented as a level-order list (using `null` for missing children), determine whether it is a valid BST.
constraints: Node values fit in 32-bit signed integers

solution.approach: DFS with valid min/max bounds for each node. Every node must be strictly between parent bounds.

problems.title: Factorial
problems.statement: Compute `n!` recursively. By definition, `0! = 1`.

solution.approach: Base case n <= 1 returns 1. Recursive case multiplies n by factorial(n-1).

problems.title: Fibonacci (Recursive)
problems.statement: Return the nth Fibonacci number using recursion. Assume F(0)=0 and F(1)=1.

solution.approach: Naive recursion: fib(n) = fib(n-1) + fib(n-2) with base cases 0 and 1.

problems.title: Merge Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using merge sort. Return the sorted array.
constraints: Target time complexity: O(n log n)

solution.approach: Recursively split the array, sort halves, then merge two sorted halves with two pointers.

problems.title: Binary Search
problems.statement: Given a sorted array of distinct integers and a target, return the index of target or -1 if not found.

solution.approach: Maintain left/right pointers. Compare mid element with target and halve the search range.

problems.title: Fibonacci (Dynamic Programming)
problems.statement: Return the nth Fibonacci number using dynamic programming (bottom-up tabulation). F(0)=0, F(1)=1.
constraints: Target space complexity: O(1) or O(n)

solution.approach: Iteratively build up from base cases using two variables for previous two values.

problems.title: Breadth-First Search Traversal
problems.statement: Given an undirected graph as an adjacency list and a starting node, return the BFS traversal order.
constraints: Nodes are labeled from 0 to n-1 or given as strings

solution.approach: Use a queue and visited set. Dequeue a node, append to result, enqueue unvisited neighbors.

problems.title: Min Heap - Insert and Extract
problems.statement: Implement a min-heap class with `insert(val)` and `extract_min()`. `peek_min()` should return the smallest element without removing it.
constraints: insert and extract_min should run in O(log n)

solution.approach: Use an array-backed heap. Bubble up on insert and bubble down on extract.

problems.title: Implement a Dynamic Array
problems.statement: Implement a `DynamicArray` class backed by a JavaScript array. Support `push(val)`, `pop()` (return removed value or null if empty), `get(index)` (return value or null if out of bounds), and `size()`.
constraints: push and pop should be O(1) amortized

solution.approach: Wrap a native array. push uses array.push, pop uses array.pop, get checks bounds.

problems.title: Implement a Hash Table
problems.statement: Implement a `HashMap` class with `set(key, value)`, `get(key)` (return value or null), `has(key)` (boolean), and `keys()` (array of keys in insertion order).
constraints: Keys and values are strings or numbers

solution.approach: Use an object/map for chaining buckets. Track key order separately for keys().

problems.title: Singly Linked List Operations
problems.statement: Implement a `SinglyLinkedList` class with `append(val)`, `prepend(val)`, `insertAt(index, val)`, `removeAt(index)`, and `toArray()`. Return null from removeAt if index is invalid.

solution.approach: Maintain head pointer. Walk nodes for index-based insert/remove.

problems.title: Doubly Linked List
problems.statement: Implement a `DoublyLinkedList` with `append(val)`, `prepend(val)`, `remove(val)` (first occurrence), and `toArray()` (head to tail).
constraints: remove returns true if removed, false otherwise

solution.approach: Use head/tail pointers and prev/next links for O(1) ends and scan for remove.

problems.title: Implement a Stack
problems.statement: Implement a `Stack` class using an array: `push(val)`, `pop()`, `peek()`, `isEmpty()`.

solution.approach: Array-backed stack using push/pop on the end.

problems.title: Implement a Queue
problems.statement: Implement a `Queue` class using an array: `enqueue(val)`, `dequeue()`, `peek()`, `isEmpty()`.

solution.approach: Use array with head index to avoid costly shift, or use simple shift for learning.

problems.title: Binary Search Tree Operations
problems.statement: Implement a `BST` class with `insert(val)`, `search(val)` (boolean), `remove(val)` (boolean), and `inorder()` (sorted array).
constraints: Average O(log n) for balanced trees, O(n) worst case

solution.approach: Classic BST recursion for insert/search; remove handles leaf, one child, and two children.

problems.title: Implement a Trie
problems.statement: Implement a `Trie` with `insert(word)`, `search(word)` (exact match), and `startsWith(prefix)`.
constraints: O(m) time where m is word length

solution.approach: Each node stores children map and isEnd flag.

problems.title: Build Adjacency List from Edges
problems.statement: Given an array of edges `[from, to]` and a boolean `directed`, return an adjacency list (object mapping node -> sorted neighbor array).
constraints: Nodes may be strings or numbers
O(E log E) or O(E) acceptable

solution.approach: For each edge, add outgoing and optionally reverse edge for undirected graphs. Sort neighbors.

problems.title: Depth-First Search Traversal
problems.statement: Given an undirected graph as an adjacency list and a start node, return DFS traversal order.

solution.approach: Recursive or iterative DFS with visited set.

problems.title: Reverse String (Recursive)
problems.statement: Reverse a string using recursion. Do not use loops.

solution.approach: Return last char + reverse of substring without last char. Base case: empty or single char.

problems.title: Bubble Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using bubble sort.

solution.approach: Repeatedly swap adjacent out-of-order elements; stop early if no swaps.

problems.title: Selection Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using selection sort.

solution.approach: Select min of unsorted suffix and swap to front each pass.

problems.title: Insertion Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using insertion sort.

solution.approach: Insert each element into the sorted prefix by shifting larger elements right.

problems.title: Quick Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using quick sort.

solution.approach: Partition around pivot, recursively sort left and right partitions.

problems.title: Heap Sort
problems.statement: Sort an integer array in ascending order using heap sort.

solution.approach: Build max heap, repeatedly extract max to end of array.

problems.title: Counting Sort
problems.statement: Sort a non-negative integer array using counting sort. Assume values are in range 0..1000.

solution.approach: Count occurrences, then rebuild output array in order.

problems.title: Linear Search
problems.statement: Return the index of `target` in `nums`, or -1 if not found. Use linear search.

solution.approach: Scan array left to right until target found.

problems.title: Binary Tree Traversals
problems.statement: Given a binary tree in level-order array form (`null` for missing nodes), return `{ preorder, inorder, postorder }` arrays.

solution.approach: DFS from root index with three collectors for pre/in/post order.

problems.title: Dijkstra Shortest Path
problems.statement: Given a weighted directed graph as `{ node: [[neighbor, weight], ...] }` and a `start` node, return shortest distances from start to all reachable nodes as an object.

solution.approach: Greedy with min-distance node selection; relax neighbors.

problems.title: Fibonacci (Memoization)
problems.statement: Return the nth Fibonacci number using top-down dynamic programming (memoization). F(0)=0, F(1)=1.

solution.approach: Recursive with memo object caching subproblem results.

problems.title: Radix Sort
problems.statement: Sort a non-negative integer array using LSD radix sort (base 10). Return a new sorted array.
constraints: O(d * (n + k)) time where d is number of digits

solution.approach: Sort by each digit place from least significant to most using counting/bucket distribution.

problems.title: Bellman-Ford Shortest Path
problems.statement: Given a weighted directed graph `{ node: [[neighbor, weight], ...] }` and a `start` node, return `{ distances, hasNegativeCycle }`. `distances[node]` is shortest distance from start, or `null` if unreachable. If a negative cycle is reachable, set `hasNegativeCycle` to true and `distances` to null.

examples.input: graph with edges A->B(1), B->C(2), start=A

solution.approach: Relax all edges V-1 times, then one more pass to detect negative cycles.

problems.title: AVL Tree Insert with Rotations
problems.statement: Implement an `AVLTree` class with `insert(val)` and `inorder()` (sorted values). The tree must stay height-balanced using LL, RR, LR, and RL rotations after each insert.
constraints: Height balance factor must stay in {-1, 0, 1}

examples.output: inorder=[20,30] wait - [20] root? Actually after AVL: 20 root, 10 left, 30 right -> [10,20,30]

solution.approach: BST insert then rebalance using single/double rotations based on balance factor and child direction.
